hook-contract 0.1.2 — domain-anchored SDK provenance

orank scored Multi-language SDK 1/3 ("found only in npm") — the headline weakness on the 83/B scan. Root cause: the scanner verifies an official SDK by domain match, but both published packages advertised GitHub, not the product domain.

Before → after (published registry metadata)

PackageRepo metadataPublished 0.1.1This release 0.1.2
@orchestkit/hook-contract (npm) no homepage field homepage → github homepage → orchestkit.yonyon.ai
orchestkit-hook-contract (PyPI) Homepage already = domain published artifact → github republish serves the domain URL

Registries serve immutable 0.1.1, so the already-correct pyproject only reaches PyPI via a new version. npm needed the homepage field added regardless (it was simply missing).

Metadata-only, parity holds

contract: 19 events, 13 typed payloads byte-identical to 0.1.1 parity: npm __version__ 0.1.2 == PyPI __version__ 0.1.2 (checked locally) schemas: untouched — no code, no test, no golden-vector change

Release-gated — the tags are the operator's to push

Publishing is an outward-facing, irreversible registry action, so this PR only stages it. After merge, the operator pushes the two tags; OIDC trusted publishing does the rest.

git tag hook-contract-py/v0.1.2 && git push origin hook-contract-py/v0.1.2 # → PyPI git tag hook-contract-npm/v0.1.2 && git push origin hook-contract-npm/v0.1.2 # → npm

Expected orank effect: Multi-language SDK 1/3 → 2/3 (+1, ~83→84). 3/3 would need Go/RubyGems SDKs that do not exist — not fabricated here.
